claim: Different LLM providers are trained with different tool formats: OpenAI models expect patch-based edits (Git diff style), Claude models expect string replacement (find-replace); using wrong format costs extra reasoning tokens and increases errors
actionable: Build provider-specific harnesses: use patch format for OpenAI, string replacement for Claude; don't force single tool format across providers
source_title: The Model Doesn't Matter. The Harness Does.

claim: Dynamic context harnesses fetch context on-demand based on agent needs rather than loading everything upfront, reducing token waste and increasing capability
actionable: Design harnesses where agents request specific context when needed rather than static upfront loading; implement context fetching as agent decision
source_title: The Model Doesn't Matter. The Harness Does.

claim: Classifying tool errors into three categories (invalid arguments, unexpected environment, provider error) enables 10x error reduction for same model by tuning harness rather than changing model
actionable: Implement error classification to distinguish model mistakes from environmental issues; use feedback to improve prompts and tool definitions, not just model
source_title: The Model Doesn't Matter. The Harness Does.

claim: Keep rate (fraction of agent-generated code that users retain after interval) is better quality metric than benchmarks; code survival indicates real-world utility
actionable: Measure agent quality via keep rate (code users keep) rather than benchmark scores; track what fraction of generated code survives user review
source_title: The Model Doesn't Matter. The Harness Does.

claim: Switching models mid-conversation reduces performance; new model applies tools to conversation history from different model (out-of-distribution); causes cache miss and slower turns
actionable: Don't switch models mid-task in chat interfaces; if switching needed, insert custom instructions warning model about tool mismatches and cache implications
source_title: The Model Doesn't Matter. The Harness Does.

claim: Multi-agent reliability compounds: 5 agents at 95% each yields only 77.4% end-to-end reliability; orchestration logic must live in harness, not in individual agents
actionable: In multi-agent systems, implement orchestration in harness layer (dispatcher, task framing, result stitching); don't rely on agents to coordinate themselves
source_title: The Model Doesn't Matter. The Harness Does.

claim: The moat for agent builders is harness craft (context strategy, tool formats, error handling, orchestration), not model access; same model with different harness scores 45-55% on benchmarks
actionable: Invest engineering effort in harness design (context fetching, tool definitions, error classification) rather than chasing model access or capabilities
source_title: The Model Doesn't Matter. The Harness Does.
